title: cyclobalanopsis oidocarpa
intro: Cyclobalanopsis oidocarpa, also known as the Chinese chestnut oak or Oidocarpa oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to China. It belongs to the Fagaceae family and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. The plant's bark, leaves, and seeds are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Cyclobalanopsis oidocarpa include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to promote overall health and well-being, and may also help to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels.
